title: Intensely Energizing Korean Pork Anju Jjigae
description: A garlicky and gooey Korean dish from the Gyeongsang region, featuring cocoa for an energizing twist, this anju jjigae is perfect for a snack, and is onion and garlic free for those with sensitivities.
instructions: Cut the pork belly into small pieces and set aside.
Mix the cocoa powder, gochujang, soy sauce, and brown sugar in a large bowl.
Add the sesame oil to the bowl and stir until well combined.
Add the pork belly, green chili peppers, and firm tofu to the bowl and stir until everything is well coated.
Transfer the mixture to a large pot and add 2 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
Stir in the toasted sesame seeds and continue to simmer for another 10 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the anju jjigae hot, garnished with additional toasted sesame seeds if desired.
